title: Robin Hood Wins the Sheriff's Golden Arrow
titleZh: 罗宾汉赢得郡长的金箭

q.en: Where did Robin Hood and his Merry Men live?
q.zh: 罗宾汉和他的快乐伙伴住在哪里？

explain.en: The book says Robin Hood lived with his band 'deep inside Sherwood Forest.'
explain.zh: 书中说，罗宾汉与他的队伍住在“舍伍德森林深处”。

q.en: In the story, the word 'duped' means...
q.zh: 在故事中，“duped”（上当）一词的意思是……

explain.zh: 词汇表将duped定义为“被欺骗”。

q.en: How did Robin Hood avoid being arrested by the Sheriff's guard?
q.zh: 罗宾汉是如何避免被郡长的卫兵逮捕的？

options.en: He fed the guard until he fell asleep and stole the warrant
options.zh: 他让卫兵吃撑到睡着，然后偷走了逮捕令

explain.en: Robin 'invited the guard to a feast'; the guard 'ate too much and fell asleep,' so Robin stole the warrant.
explain.zh: 罗宾汉“邀请卫兵赴宴”；卫兵“吃得太多睡着了”，于是罗宾偷走了逮捕令。

q.en: Why did Robin disguise himself as a beggar?
q.zh: 罗宾汉为什么要伪装成乞丐？

options.en: So the Sheriff would not recognize him at the archery festival
options.zh: 为了不让郡长在射箭比赛上认出他

explain.en: Robin colored his hair with walnut stain and wore a patch and ragged clothes so the Sheriff 'did not recognize him.'
explain.zh: 罗宾汉用核桃渍染发、戴上眼罩、穿上破衣，因此郡长“没有认出他”。

q.en: Who won the gold-covered arrow at the archery festival?
q.zh: 在射箭比赛上，谁赢得了镀金箭？

explain.en: The 'tattered beggar'—Robin in disguise—shot the center and 'won the gold-covered arrow.'
explain.zh: 那个“衣衫褴褛的乞丐”（伪装的罗宾）射中靶心，“赢得了镀金箭”。

q.en: Why did the Sheriff hold the archery festival?
q.zh: 郡长为什么要举办射箭比赛？

options.en: To trap and catch Robin Hood
options.zh: 为了设陷阱抓住罗宾汉

explain.en: The Sheriff said, 'I'll catch him by trickery... When he comes, I'll arrest and hang him.'
explain.zh: 郡长说：“我要用计谋抓住他……等他一来，我就逮捕并绞死他。”

q.en: What trait best describes Robin Hood in this tale?
q.zh: 在故事中，哪个特质最能描述罗宾汉？

options.en: A clever outlaw who helps the poor
options.zh: 一个帮助穷人的聪明亡命之徒

explain.en: Robin 'stole back the gold taken in unjust taxes' and gave it to the poor, and used clever disguises.
explain.zh: 罗宾汉“夺回被不公征税夺走的金子”分给穷人，并使用了巧妙的伪装。

q.en: How did the common people and the Sheriff's enemies view Robin differently?
q.zh: 普通百姓和郡长的敌人对罗宾的看法有何不同？

options.en: Enemies saw him as a hated robber; common folk saw him as a beloved hero
options.zh: 敌人视他为可恨的强盗；百姓视他为受人爱戴的英雄

explain.en: The book says, 'To his enemies, Robin and his men were hated robbers. To the common folk they helped, they were beloved heroes.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“对敌人而言，罗宾和他的手下是可恨的强盗；对被他们帮助的百姓而言，他们是受人爱戴的英雄。”

q.en: How did Robin let the Sheriff know he had won?
q.zh: 罗宾汉如何让郡长知道是他赢了？

options.en: He sent a letter by mail
He shot an arrow with a note through the window into the Sheriff's roast
options.zh: 他射出一支带字条的箭，穿过窗户扎进郡长的烤肉里

explain.en: A 'shaft flew through the window. It stuck straight into the roast' with a note saying the beggar was Robin Hood.
explain.zh: “一支箭飞进窗户，直直扎进烤肉里”，附带的字条说明那个乞丐就是罗宾汉。

q.en: What kind of story is this, and what does it admire?
q.zh: 这是一个关于什么的故事，它赞美了什么？

options.zh: 一篇关于射箭的新闻报道

options.en: A folktale that admires Robin's cleverness and fairness to the poor
options.zh: 一个赞美罗宾的机智和对穷人公平的民间传说

explain.en: It is 'An English Folktale' that celebrates Robin Hood's cleverness and his giving to the poor.
explain.zh: 这是“一则英国民间传说”，歌颂了罗宾汉的机智以及他分给穷人的义举。

prompt.en: Robin Hood gave gold back to the poor. Write about a time you think it is fair to share or give something to someone else.
prompt.zh: 罗宾汉把金子还给了穷人。写一写你认为把东西分享或赠予他人是公平合理的一种情形。

en: Describe the situation you have in mind.
Explain why sharing or giving was the right thing to do.
zh: 解释为什么分享或给予是正确的做法。
